You are a UI/UX product expert generating revision task specifications for software engineers. You will be shown a screenshot of a mobile or web UI and asked to generate a concrete revision task.

SCOPE GUIDELINES:
Each task should be a minor, targeted revision — roughly one incremental step above the current state of the UI (think n → n+1, not n → n+10). A good task touches one to three components. Avoid:
  • Trivially cosmetic changes (e.g. shifting one element by a pixel).
  • Sweeping redesigns that restructure the entire layout or replace multiple sections of the page at once.

EACH TASK MUST CONTAIN ALL THREE OF THE FOLLOWING:
  (a) Motivation: Why is this change needed? Explain what is currently suboptimal or what goal the revision serves, framed in terms of the revision type specified in the prompt.
  (b) Precise component identification: Name the specific component(s) to be changed and describe their current appearance and location in the screenshot (e.g. 'the dark blue primary action button labeled "Continue" in the bottom center of the screen').
  (c) Unambiguous change description: Describe exactly what the component should look like or do after the revision. Leave no room for interpretation — if a color is changing, say which color; if text is moving, say where it should end up.

EXAMPLE OUTPUT (category: Clarify Function & State):
The "Continue" button is fully green and active even though no departure point has been selected yet. This could lead to errors if the user taps Continue without selecting anything. Set the Continue button to a disabled/muted state by using a faint green at 20% opacity instead of the full green.

OUTPUT FORMAT:
Return a single revision task as a short, self-contained paragraph of two to four sentences. No preamble, headers, or closing remarks — only the task text.

REVISION TYPE — your task must belong to this category:
  Refine Layout & Spacing: Adjusts the low-level alignment, spacing, sizing, or composition of UI elements for better visual balance and structure.

Generate the revision task for the UI shown in the screenshot.

Consider the overlaid text and badge elements sitting on the cover photo, from the username down to the role labels. The user ID, badges (40, 11, Reach), role labels ("Talent Singer" and "Party Guru of Room 237369"), and username are all layered directly on top of the cover photo, making them difficult to read against the busy photographic background and creating a cluttered, hard-to-scan profile header. Keep the profile icon on the cover photo and introduce a dedicated content section below the cover photo where the username, ID, badges, and role labels sit on a clean solid background, improving legibility and giving each element room to breathe.

Generate the revision task for the UI shown in the screenshot.

The "Follow" and "Chat" buttons are pinned to the bottom of the screen, visually disconnected from the profile identity section they relate to. A user has to scroll past stats, a group card, and navigation tabs before reaching the primary actions, which delays the most important decision on a profile page. Move the "Follow" and "Chat" buttons to sit directly below the stats row. Restructure the full profile layout in this order: cover photo and avatar, then username, ID, badges and role labels on a clean background, then the stats row, then the Follow and Chat buttons, then the group card, then Personal Info, then the Homepage, Moment, Playlist and Covers tab navigation at the bottom. This ensures the user sees who the person is, how popular they are, and how to connect with them before being presented with any content to browse.

Generate the revision task for the UI shown in the screenshot.

Consider the action button row beneath the Arby's and Open 24/7 details in the header card. The Call, Directions, Info, and Share action buttons are disproportionately large relative to the rest of the UI, making them feel heavy and out of scale with the compact information-dense layout above and below them. Scale the buttons down - reduce the circle size and icon size proportionally - and ensure equal spacing between all four buttons with the group centre-aligned as a whole within the card width. The labels beneath each button should scale down accordingly to maintain the same visual relationship between icon and label.

Generate the revision task for the UI shown in the screenshot.

There is no visual indication on the fuel list that DIESEL #2 has been selected or will be the item paid for when tapping "Love's Pay." Neither the DEF nor the DIESEL #2 row shows a selected state — no highlight, checkmark, or active indicator - leaving the user uncertain about what they are actually paying for. Add a coloured left border to show a clear selected state to the DIESEL #2 row. The button should read "Pay for Diesel #2" rather than the generic "Love's Pay".

Generate the revision task for the UI shown in the screenshot.

The header area has two separate navigation bars stacked on top of each other - a yellow bar with Facebook and Instagram icons, and a white bar with cart, profile, and menu icons - creating a heavy, cluttered top section that takes up too much vertical space. Consolidate both bars into a single header bar, grouping the social icons and utility icons on the same row with consistent spacing, to reduce the header height and give more space to the content below.

The course title "Guided Meditation: Abundance" is centre-aligned, while the Teacher and Category information below it is also centre-aligned, but the category thumbnail image breaks that alignment by introducing a left-anchored element, creating an inconsistent layout. Fully centre-align the Teacher and Category rows as consistent metadata to match the title above and also keep the thumbnail within the Category section centre-aligned.

Generate the revision task for the UI shown in the screenshot.

The typography hierarchy in the card is flat, specifically on the card body. The post title should change to a font weight of 700 and be bold. The subreddit name should retain the blue colour and be set to 12sp. The username should be 12sp with a black colour at 60% opacity and sit 2sp below the headline. The timestamp and comments should be reduced to 10sp in size, be light, sit flush together at 0sp gap, be 2sp below the subreddit name and username row, and have a 4sp margin-bottom before the button section. The award icon should be scaled to 10×10px and the award count text set to 10sp, both aligned to the right of the comments row on the same line. The title needs to lead more boldly, and the metadata below it needs to be visually quieter — smaller, lighter, and more muted across all three tiers.

Generate the revision task for the UI shown in the screenshot.

The yellow lines crossing the map appear to be major roads or distribution routes, but there is no legend, label, or tooltip confirming this. A user unfamiliar with French road maps would have no idea what the yellow lines represent or whether they are relevant to the distributor locations. Either label the yellow lines explicitly as roads with their names, or add a map legend that explains what yellow, grey, and other line colors mean in the context of this distributor app specifically.

Generate the revision task for the UI shown in the screenshot.

There is no "you are here" dot or blue location marker on the map. The user cannot orient themselves relative to the distributor pins, making distance and direction impossible to judge. Show the user's current GPS location as a standard blue dot so they can immediately understand their proximity to each distributor.
